_provenance: band_levels_db are band-averages relative to the 500Hz-1kHz anchor (=0). Each target is tagged with the RIG it is defined on (iec711 / gras_43ag / bk5128) and a confidence. RIGS ARE NOT INTERCHANGEABLE: a device curve must be compared to a target on the SAME rig (5128 reads ~+6dB hotter at 8kHz and avoids the 711's 10-14kHz dip). Sources: AutoEq canonical target CSVs (Harman OE 2013/2015/2018, IE 2019, 5128 DF), Crinacle/Headphones.com (JM-1, IEF, 5128 gold-standard), Olive/Harman AES. See research-bibliography.md.

bk5128_ff.label: B&K 5128 Free Field (illustrative)
bk5128_ff._note: rarely used as a tuning target; sharper ~3kHz peak, darker upper treble than DF; directional, no canonical raw file located

vince_iem_ref.label: Vince IEM reference (JM-1 -0.6dB/oct +4dB bass)
vince_iem_ref._construction: JM-1 (5128 DF-based neutral baseline) + a -0.6 dB/oct downward tilt (gain = -0.6*log2(f/1000), pivot 1kHz) + a +4dB bass low-shelf (~105Hz corner: +4 sub_bass, +3 mid_bass, +0.5 lower_mids), then re-anchored to center_mids=0. Vince's personal IEM reference; a mild-bass, gently down-tilted target. Verify the bass-shelf corner/shape and tilt low-end against your own tool (Vince cited +2.9dB @20Hz, ~ -0.52dB/oct or a bass-flattened tilt).
